title: cenocline cinerea
intro: Cenocline cinerea, commonly known as the ash-colored forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species in the borage family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Cenocline cinerea include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as a natural sedative. Its bioactive constituents include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Cenocline cinerea can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. If ingested in excessive amounts, it can cause gastrointestinal problems, including vomiting and diarrhea. In rare cases, improper use of cenocline cinerea may lead to interactions with other medications, resulting in adverse health effects. Additionally, skin irritation and respiratory issues may occur if the plant's extracts come into contact with the skin or are inhaled.
